title: Stanford AI Index 2026: China Has 'Nearly Erased' US AI Lead — Gap Shrinks from 1,300 to 39 Arena Points
detail: Stanford University's Human-Centered AI Institute released its landmark 2026 AI Index report on April 16, delivering a striking finding: China has 'nearly erased' the United States' artificial intelligence performance lead. In May 2023, GPT-4 led China's best models by more than 1,300 points on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena benchmark. By March 2026, that gap had shrunk to just 39 points — Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 leads China's Dola-Seed 2.0 by only 2.7%. Additional findings: China surpassed the US in AI research publication citation share (20.6% vs. 12.6%); China leads in industrial robotics deployment (~295,000 installations vs. US 34,200); the flow of AI scholars moving to the US dropped 89% since 2017, with an 80% decline in just the past year. DeepSeek's R1 and V3 models were specifically cited as evidence of China's accelerating competitive position. The Stanford findings directly challenged the US national security community's assumption that export controls are effectively widening the US-China AI performance gap — the data indicates the gap has narrowed dramatically despite escalating chip sanctions.

media.caption: Stanford 2026 AI Index: China has nearly erased the US AI performance lead, closing from 1,300 to just 39 Arena benchmark points since 2023

title: US Congress: China Uses 'Buy What It Can, Steal What It Must' Strategy to Acquire Frontier AI Chips
detail: The House Select Committee on the Chinese Communist Party released a comprehensive investigation report on April 16 titled 'Buy What It Can, Steal What It Must: China's Campaign to Acquire Frontier AI Capabilities.' The report details how China systematically uses both legal procurement (stockpiling before controls take effect), gray market channels (transshipment through third countries), and outright theft to acquire US semiconductor technology and frontier AI capabilities. Key findings: China routed billions in Nvidia GPU purchases through intermediaries in Taiwan, Thailand, Hong Kong, and the UAE; Chinese entities have acquired AI chip servers worth at least $92 million in banned Nvidia hardware disclosed to Beijing regulators; model distillation techniques allow Chinese labs to extract capabilities from US AI models without direct hardware access. The report noted that China's acquisition campaign has accelerated significantly since the October 2022 export controls, with the dollar value of chips acquired through gray channels estimated to have increased 300%+ since restrictions took effect.

sources.name: House Select Committee on the CCP

media.caption: House Select Committee investigation reveals China's systematic use of legal procurement and smuggling to acquire frontier AI chips despite US export controls
media.source: House Select Committee on the CCP
